changelog: FIRST-LAUNCH PICKER — Pro is now the default. Always shown FIRST and always pre-selected (focused) when the dialog opens. Lite is the fallback for older / built-in TVs.

DEVICE-CAPABILITY DETECTOR REWRITTEN — was reading ActivityManager.getMemoryClass() which returns the per-app HEAP LIMIT (often 96–192MB even on a NVIDIA Shield with 3GB of RAM). That's why the Shield was being mis-classified as Lite. Now reads ActivityManager.MemoryInfo.totalMem (actual system RAM) and treats any device from NVIDIA / Amazon / Google / Onn / Xiaomi / Roku / TiVo as Pro by default. Generic builds need ≥ 2 GB RAM and ≥ 4 cores to default to Pro.

PLAIN-ENGLISH COPY — 'Pick the version that fits your TV. Most people want Pro. If your TV feels slow with Pro, switch to Lite anytime in Settings.' Pro tagline mentions Fire Stick 4K, NVIDIA Shield, Chromecast with Google TV, Onn 4K Pro by name. Lite tagline names Hisense / TCL / Sceptre as the kind of built-in smart TV it's for.
